description: A vertical designed 'Labor News' graphic combining a photograph with a headline overlay in the DOL brand style. The photo is a dramatic low-angle shot of the bow of a large cargo/freight ship (dark hull above a red-painted lower hull) in a dry dock or shipyard, with industrial gantry structures at left and an overcast sky. A faint watermark of the circular Department of Labor seal ('...ARTMENT OF...') is ghosted over the lower portion of the image. Across the bottom-left, a blue tab with white italic text reads 'LABOR NEWS' (with three short vertical accent bars), beneath which a bold white serif headline states 'US Department of Labor eases regulatory burden for vital industries, protecting workers & cutting costs.' The lower right carries 'DOL.GOV' in white. The style is a polished branded news-banner/headline card. Messaging technique: positive policy promotion framing deregulation (here, easing burdens under the Longshore & Harbor Workers' Compensation Act for shipbuilding/energy/defense) as simultaneously protecting workers and cutting costs. Target audience: industry stakeholders and the general public.
notable_text: LABOR NEWS / US Department of Labor eases regulatory burden for vital industries, protecting workers & cutting costs / DOL.GOV
